title: vicia tenuifolia subsp. atroviolacea
intro: Vicia tenuifolia subsp. atroviolacea, also known as purple vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and act as a natural remedy for various kidney and urinary issues. Vicia tenuifolia subsp. atroviolacea can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
